subject: Accounting
title: Railroads and Cost Accounting
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Railroads and Cost Accounting.

hook.text: Railroads in the 1850s were the largest companies ever built. They needed new accounting.

buildup.text: Tracking costs across thousands of miles of track, hundreds of stations, and millions of passengers.

discovery.text: Railroad managers invented cost accounting—breaking expenses by route, train, and department.

twist.text: For the first time, managers could see which routes made money and which ones lost it.

climax.text: Cost accounting techniques from railroads spread to factories and became standard practice.

punchline.text: Railroads invented the accounting that factories needed.

quiz.question: What did railroads pioneer in accounting?
options: Cost accounting by department and route
